Agent timed out / ran out of steps with p=0.500. Evidence for: []. Evidence against: [].

The question asks whether there will be at least one day before June 1, 2025, when the word "tariff(s)" does not appear on the front print pages of both The New York Times and the Wall Street Journal. Because the resolution criteria only require a single day for the question to resolve as "Yes" (i.e. the streak of the word appearing in at least one paper must break), the odds strongly favor a "Yes". Sustaining a specific word on the front page of at least one of these papers every single day for weeks or months is incredibly difficult, even during a presidency heavy on trade news. Furthermore, the WSJ does not publish a Sunday print edition, meaning that every Sunday the requirement falls entirely on the NYT to contain the word "tariff" somewhere on its front page. It is highly probable that on at least one day—most likely a weekend—neither paper's front page mentions the word prior to the June 1, 2025 cutoff.
